title: Disappearing images after successful podman build
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

This looks quite weired to me. I upgraded my system from Fedora F31 to F32 and i got a strange behaviour.
Whenever i build a new podman images, i see the newly created image on a different console. When the
podman build has been successfully finished, the images stays visible `podman images`  for a short 
period of time an then disappear.

I have no clue what is going on. In order to access a nexus repo, i am using `FROM image on repo` followed by a `copy ..`. I thought that podman is trying to send the newly created image to nexus, but i do not see any activity,

The system has been updater from F31 to F32. Maybe the FS of podman is broken.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  create a new image via podman

2. check immediately if image is there `podman images` 

3. wait a while and see that the image disappared 

**Describe the results you received:**


**Describe the results you expected:**


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

comments.body: We have not heard about this from anyone else, so I feel like something strange might be going on on your system.

Salut Daniel  
thank you for your opinion. You must be right, the dev-Environement was an ole F31, that has been upgraded. Later i change the OCI runtime environement to podman, that was accidentally set to an alternate implementation. It would be a waste to figure out, what went wrong.
I installed a new F32 server and the problem disappeared - maybe a bad ole package, i dunno.

Thank you and good luck to you!
Christian
